title: Glandularia paulensis
taxonomy: Glandularia paulensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. It belongs to the genus Glandularia, a group of plants commonly known as rosy verbena. The classification of Glandularia paulensis is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. Glandularia paulensis is also classified as a member of the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification provides a clear understanding of Glandularia paulensis' evolutionary history and its relationships with other plant species.

intro: Glandularia paulensis is a flowering plant species native to the Andes mountains, belonging to the verbena family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to the relief of symptoms associated with digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Glandularia paulensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions due to its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties.
